must_detect.matched_text: Term Sheet for Proposed Acquisition by Seri Pelanduk Berhad (Bursa Main Market issuer) of 62% equity in Orkid Foods Berhad from Pelita Holdings Sdn Bhd.
Financing covenants: Net Debt/EBITDA ≤ 3.25x post-closing; dividend stopper until pro forma cash balance exceeds RM 60,000,000.
Negative assurance: This term sheet is not, and does not contain, a profit forecast or projection and should not be relied upon by public investors.

STRICTLY PRIVATE & CONFIDENTIAL — NON-PUBLIC; Deal Codename: Project Cempaka; Embargo until 15 July 2026, 17:00 MYT or Bursa disclosure, whichever is earlier.
Term Sheet for Proposed Acquisition by Seri Pelanduk Berhad (Bursa Main Market issuer) of 62% equity in Orkid Foods Berhad from Pelita Holdings Sdn Bhd.
Consideration: RM 280,000,000 cash, funded by RM 180,000,000 senior term facility from Meranti Bank Berhad and RM 100,000,000 internal cash.
Indicative valuation implies RM 1.75 per Orkid Foods Berhad share, representing a 22% premium to the 30-day VWAP based on public trading data.
Conditions precedent include Securities Commission Malaysia clearance under the Malaysian Code on Take-overs and Mergers 2016, Bursa Malaysia Main Market Listing Requirements Chapter 10 approvals, and shareholders’ approval by ordinary resolution.
Definitive agreements: Share Sale Agreement and Ancillary Transitional Services Agreement to be executed by 30 June 2026, with a long-stop date of 30 September 2026.
MAC/MAE: No Material Adverse Change or Effect shall have occurred to Orkid Foods Berhad since 31 March 2026; for the avoidance of doubt, routine seasonal raw material cost swings previously disclosed shall not constitute a MAC.
Key individuals: Dato' Hisham Razali (Chairman, Seri Pelanduk Berhad) and Mr. Chan Wei Jian (CEO, Orkid Foods Berhad); contact for queries: Puan Nur Liyana Idris, CFO, liyana.idris@seripelanduk.com, +60 3-8899 0044.
Financing covenants: Net Debt/EBITDA ≤ 3.25x post-closing; dividend stopper until pro forma cash balance exceeds RM 60,000,000.
Confidentiality and no-announcement: Parties shall not make any public statement or Bursa filing until the embargo lifts; a draft IR release exists but is not to be uploaded to the Bursa platform yet.
Research context: industry trends cited from Kelapa Institute 2024 Food Processing Survey (public source, non-issuer) and general commodity prices from publicly available sources.
Negative assurance: This term sheet is not, and does not contain, a profit forecast or projection and should not be relied upon by public investors.
project management note (internal): target dd close friday; please send latest vendor list; spa-day voucher raffle is postponed to august.
Break fee: RM 6,000,000 payable by buyer only if failure to obtain internal approvals; no reverse break fee.
